title: hieracium guthnikianum subsp. naegelii
intro: Hieracium guthnikianum subsp. naegelii, also known as a type of hawkweed, is a plant species within the dandelion-like family Asteraceae. This plant is said to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its leaves and roots, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits associated with Hieracium guthnikianum subsp. naegelii include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Traditionally, the plant's herbal preparations involve infusions of its leaves or roots, which can be used as teas or applied topically as a poultice or ointment.
